content:
Member-only story

5 min read
Just now

--

The SDLC you learned in college is no longer the one your engineering team follows.
Every engineer knows the Software Development Lifecycle.
Requirements.
Design.
Implementation.
Testing.
Deployment.
Five phases that have shaped software engineering for decades.
It made perfect sense when the hardest part of building software was writing code.
But look around your team today.
How much time do engineers actually spend typing code?
Probably less than you think.
Instead, they are:

• Writing prompts for AI

• Reviewing AI-generated pull requests

• Validating architectural decisions

• Running automated quality gates

• Learning from production telemetry

• Feeding those insights back into the next iteration
The software development lifecycle didn’t disappear.
It quietly evolved.
And most engineering teams are already following an eight-phase SDLC without realizing it.
